---
title: "ACTL4001 Equipment Failure"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-18"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(readxl)
Frequency <- read_excel("Frequency.xlsx")
View(Frequency)
library(readxl)
Severity <- read_excel("Severity.xlsx")
View(Severity)

# Determining appropriate frequency model - Poisson was chosen
mean_freq <- mean(Frequency$claim_count, na.rm = TRUE)
var_freq  <- var(Frequency$claim_count, na.rm = TRUE)

mean_freq
var_freq
library(MASS)

# Poisson model
pois_model <- glm(claim_count ~ 1, family = poisson, data = Frequency)

# Negative Binomial model
nb_model <- glm.nb(claim_count ~ 1, data = Frequency)
AIC(pois_model, nb_model)
dispersion <- sum(residuals(pois_model, type = "pearson")^2) / pois_model$df.residual
dispersion

# Determining appropriate severity model - Log-normal was chosen

hist(Severity$claim_amount, breaks = 50)
hist(log(Severity$claim_amount), breaks = 50)

# Add age bands to frequency and severity
# Assuming Frequency and Severity are already loaded
library(dplyr)

Frequency <- Frequency %>%
  mutate(age_band_cat = case_when(
    equipment_age < 5  ~ "0-4",
    equipment_age < 10 ~ "5-9",
    equipment_age >= 10 ~ "10+"
  ))

Severity <- Severity %>%
  mutate(age_band_cat = case_when(
    equipment_age < 5  ~ "0-4",
    equipment_age < 10 ~ "5-9",
    equipment_age >= 10 ~ "10+"
  ))

library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

Frequency <- Frequency %>%
  mutate(equipment_type2 = str_remove(equipment_type, "_.*$"))

Severity <- Severity %>%
  mutate(equipment_type2 = str_remove(equipment_type, "_.*$"))

# Clean solar system
Frequency <- Frequency %>%
  mutate(solar_system2 = str_remove(solar_system, "_.*$"))

Severity <- Severity %>%
  mutate(solar_system2 = str_remove(solar_system, "_.*$"))
```


```{r cars}
### Aggregate Loss Modelling
## Upload equipment data
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

equipment_types <- c("Quantum Bore", "Graviton Extractor", "FexStram Carrier", 
                     "ReglAggregators", "Flux Rider", "Ion Pulverizer")
solar_systems <- c("Helionis Cluster", "Bayesian System", "Oryn Delta")
age_bands <- c("<5", "5-9", "10-14", "15-19", "20+")

helionis <- data.frame(
  equipment = equipment_types,
  `<5` = c(30, 24, 15, 30, 150, 9),
  `5-9` = c(45, 36, 23, 45, 225, 14),
  `10-14` = c(180, 144, 89, 180, 900, 53),
  `15-19` = c(30, 24, 15, 30, 150, 9),
  `20+` = c(15, 12, 8, 15, 75, 5)
) %>% mutate(solar_system = "Helionis Cluster")

bayesia <- data.frame(
  equipment = equipment_types,
  `<5` = c(45, 36, 23, 45, 225, 14),
  `5-9` = c(38, 30, 19, 38, 187, 11),
  `10-14` = c(59, 48, 29, 59, 300, 18),
  `15-19` = c(8, 6, 4, 8, 38, 2),
  `20+` = c(0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0)
) %>% mutate(solar_system = "Bayesian System")

oryn <- data.frame(
  equipment = equipment_types,
  `<5` = c(75, 60, 37, 75, 375, 22),
  `5-9` = c(15, 12, 8, 15, 75, 5),
  `10-14` = c(10, 8, 5, 10, 50, 3),
  `15-19` = c(0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0),
  `20+` = c(0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0)
) %>% mutate(solar_system = "Oryn Delta")

counts_long <- bind_rows(helionis, bayesia, oryn) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -c(equipment, solar_system),
               names_to = "age_band",
               values_to = "count") %>%
  mutate(age_band = case_when(
    age_band == "X.5"   ~ "<5",
    age_band == "X5.9"  ~ "5-9",
    age_band == "X10.14" ~ "10-14",
    age_band == "X15.19" ~ "15-19",
    age_band == "X20."   ~ "20+",
    age_band == "X20"    ~ "20+",
    TRUE ~ age_band
  ))

op_pct <- data.frame(
  equipment = equipment_types,
  Helionis.Cluster = c(0.95, 0.95, 0.90, 0.80, 0.80, 0.50),
  Bayesian.System = c(0.80, 0.80, 0.75, 0.75, 0.80, 0.60),
  Oryn.Delta = c(0.75, 0.75, 0.70, 0.70, 0.75, 0.50)
) %>% pivot_longer(cols = -equipment,
                   names_to = "solar_system",
                   values_to = "op_pct")

maint <- data.frame(
  equipment = equipment_types,
  Helionis.Cluster = c(750, 750, 375, 1500, 1500, 1000),
  Bayesian.System = c(600, 600, 400, 1000, 1000, 750),
  Oryn.Delta = c(500, 500, 250, 300, 300, 500)
) %>% pivot_longer(cols = -equipment,
                   names_to = "solar_system",
                   values_to = "maintenance_int")

op_pct$solar_system <- recode(op_pct$solar_system,
                               Helionis.Cluster = "Helionis Cluster",
                               Bayesian.System = "Bayesian System",
                               Oryn.Delta = "Oryn Delta")
maint$solar_system <- recode(maint$solar_system,
                              Helionis.Cluster = "Helionis Cluster",
                              Bayesian.System = "Bayesian System",
                              Oryn.Delta = "Oryn Delta")

exposure_df <- counts_long %>%
  left_join(op_pct, by = c("equipment", "solar_system")) %>%
  left_join(maint, by = c("equipment", "solar_system")) %>%
  mutate(
    exposure = count * op_pct,
    usage_int = op_pct * 24,
    age_band_cat = case_when(
      age_band %in% c("<5")                ~ "0-4",
      age_band %in% c("5-9")               ~ "5-9",
      age_band %in% c("10-14", "15-19", "20+") ~ "10+"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(count > 0) %>%
  rename(equipment_type2 = equipment,
         solar_system2 = solar_system)

head(exposure_df)
View(exposure_df)
```


```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
# Frequency + Severity Models
# Frequency model (Poisson with offset)
freq_glm <- glm(claim_count ~ equipment_type2 + age_band_cat + solar_system2 + 
                usage_int + maintenance_int + offset(log(exposure)),
                family = poisson, data = Frequency)

# Check for overdispersion
summary(freq_glm)

# Severity model (log‑normal via linear regression on log(amount))
sev_lm <- lm(log(claim_amount) ~ equipment_type2 + age_band_cat + solar_system2 +
               usage_int + maintenance_int, data = Severity)
summary(sev_lm)
sdlog <- sigma(sev_lm)# constant standard deviation
show(sdlog)
```
```{r}
# --- 1. Recode solar systems to match model levels ---
exposure_df <- exposure_df %>%
  mutate(
    solar_system2 = recode(solar_system2,
                           "Bayesian System" = "Epsilon",
                           "Oryn Delta" = "Zeta")
  )

# --- 2. Ensure factor levels match the model's training levels ---
ss_levels <- freq_glm$xlevels[["solar_system2"]]
exposure_df$solar_system2 <- factor(exposure_df$solar_system2, levels = ss_levels)

eq_levels <- freq_glm$xlevels[["equipment_type2"]]
exposure_df$equipment_type2 <- factor(exposure_df$equipment_type2, levels = eq_levels)

# --- 3. Predict frequency, severity parameters, and total expected claims ---
exposure_df$lambda <- predict(freq_glm, newdata = exposure_df, type = "response")
exposure_df$meanlog <- predict(sev_lm, newdata = exposure_df)
exposure_df$sdlog <- sdlog   # constant from severity model

total_expected <- sum(exposure_df$lambda)               # expected total claims
exposure_df$prob <- exposure_df$lambda / total_expected # cell probability

# --- 4. Simulation ---
n_sim <- 0000
agg_loss <- numeric(n_sim)
set.seed(123)
pb <- txtProgressBar(min = 0, max = n_sim, style = 3)
for (i in 1:n_sim) {
  N <- rpois(1, total_expected)
  if (N == 0) {
    agg_loss[i] <- 0
  } else {
    cells <- sample(1:nrow(exposure_df), size = N, replace = TRUE, prob = exposure_df$prob)
    sev <- rlnorm(N, meanlog = exposure_df$meanlog[cells], sdlog = sdlog)
    agg_loss[i] <- sum(sev)
  }
}
  if (i %% 100 == 0) setTxtProgressBar(pb, i)

close(pb)
# --- 5. Summary statistics ---
mean_loss <- mean(agg_loss)
sd_loss <- sd(agg_loss)
quantiles <- quantile(agg_loss, probs = c(0.95, 0.99, 0.995, 0.999))
tvar_99 <- mean(agg_loss[agg_loss > quantile(agg_loss, 0.99)])

cat("Mean aggregate loss: ", mean_loss, "\n")
cat("Standard deviation:  ", sd_loss, "\n")
cat("95% VaR:             ", quantiles[1], "\n")
cat("99% VaR:             ", quantiles[2], "\n")
cat("99.5% VaR:           ", quantiles[3], "\n")
cat("99.9% VaR:           ", quantiles[4], "\n")
cat("TVaR (99%):          ", tvar_99, "\n")
```

```{r}
## Inflation and spot rate data
# ---- Economic data and averages ----
economic_data <- data.frame(
  Year = 2160:2174,
  Inflation = c(3.77, 2.32, 1.48, 1.08, 0.22, 0.71, 1.55, 2.16, 1.81, 0.73, 2.94, 7.08, 5.98, 2.76, 2.39),
  Rate1Y = c(0.24, 0.24, 0.19, 0.17, 0.40, 0.72, 1.42, 2.18, 2.30, 0.43, 0.15, 2.94, 5.45, 5.28, 4.74),
  Rate10Y = c(3.11, 2.08, 2.73, 2.87, 2.43, 2.09, 2.70, 3.26, 2.40, 0.95, 1.50, 3.09, 4.33, 4.80, 5.10)
)

avg_inflation <- mean(economic_data$Inflation) / 100
avg_rate1Y <- mean(economic_data$Rate1Y) / 100
avg_rate10Y <- mean(economic_data$Rate10Y) / 100

# Interpolate spot rates for years 1 to 10
max_year <- 10
spot_rates <- numeric(max_year)
for (t in 1:max_year) {
  if (t == 1) {
    spot_rates[t] <- avg_rate1Y
  } else if (t >= max_year) {
    spot_rates[t] <- avg_rate10Y
  } else {
    r1 <- log(1 + avg_rate1Y)
    r10 <- log(1 + avg_rate10Y)
    r <- r1 + (r10 - r1) * (t - 1) / (max_year - 1)
    spot_rates[t] <- exp(r) - 1
  }
}

# ---- 1‑year simulation (assuming you already have agg_loss) ----
# agg_loss is from earlier simulation (undiscounted, base year dollars)
# Inflate and discount
pv_agg_loss_1y <- agg_loss * (1 + avg_inflation) / (1 + spot_rates[1])

# Premium (VaR₉₅ of undiscounted losses)
premium_annual <- quantile(agg_loss, 0.95)
pv_premium_1y <- premium_annual
net_revenue_1y <- pv_premium_1y - pv_agg_loss_1y

# ---- Multi‑year simulation (e.g., 5 years) ----
T <- 5
n_sim <- 100000
set.seed(123)

pv_loss_5y <- numeric(n_sim)
for (i in 1:n_sim) {
  total_pv <- 0
  for (t in 1:T) {
    N <- rpois(1, total_expected)
    if (N > 0) {
      cells <- sample(1:nrow(exposure_df), size = N, replace = TRUE, prob = exposure_df$prob)
      sev <- rlnorm(N, meanlog = exposure_df$meanlog[cells], sdlog = sdlog)
      sev <- sev * (1 + avg_inflation)^t
      disc <- 1 / (1 + spot_rates[t])^t
      total_pv <- total_pv + sum(sev * disc)
    }
  }
  pv_loss_5y[i] <- total_pv
}

# Present value of premium stream for 5 years
pv_premium_5y <- sum(premium_annual / (1 + spot_rates[0:(T-1)])^(0:(T-1)))
net_revenue_5y <- pv_premium_5y - pv_loss_5y

# ---- Summary statistics ----
# 1-year
mean_loss_1y <- mean(agg_loss)
sd_loss_1y <- sd(agg_loss)
quantiles_loss_1y <- quantile(agg_loss, c(0.95, 0.99, 0.995, 0.999))
tvar_99_loss_1y <- mean(agg_loss[agg_loss > quantile(agg_loss, 0.99)])

mean_nr_1y <- mean(net_revenue_1y)
sd_nr_1y <- sd(net_revenue_1y)
prob_neg_nr_1y <- mean(net_revenue_1y < 0)

# 5-year
mean_loss_5y <- mean(pv_loss_5y)
sd_loss_5y <- sd(pv_loss_5y)
quantiles_loss_5y <- quantile(pv_loss_5y, c(0.95, 0.99, 0.995, 0.999))
tvar_99_loss_5y <- mean(pv_loss_5y[pv_loss_5y > quantile(pv_loss_5y, 0.99)])

mean_nr_5y <- mean(net_revenue_5y)
sd_nr_5y <- sd(net_revenue_5y)
prob_neg_nr_5y <- mean(net_revenue_5y < 0)

# Print or store in a data frame for reporting
# ============================================================================
# Function to compute summary statistics for a vector of values
# ============================================================================
summarize_vec <- function(x, prefix = "Loss", probs = c(0.95, 0.99, 0.995, 0.999), var_prob = 0.99) {
  stats <- c(
    mean(x),
    sd(x),
    quantile(x, probs[1]),
    quantile(x, probs[2]),
    quantile(x, probs[3]),
    quantile(x, probs[4]),
    mean(x[x > quantile(x, var_prob)])   # TVaR at var_prob
  )
  names(stats) <- c("Mean", "SD", "VaR_95", "VaR_99", "VaR_99.5", "VaR_99.9", paste0("TVaR_", var_prob*100))
  return(stats)
}
# ---- 10‑year simulation ----
T <- 10
n_sim <- 100000
set.seed(123)  # optional: use same seed for consistency
pb <- txtProgressBar(min = 0, max = n_sim, style = 3)
pv_loss_10y <- numeric(n_sim)
for (i in 1:n_sim) {
  total_pv <- 0
  for (t in 1:T) {
    N <- rpois(1, total_expected)
    if (N > 0) {
      cells <- sample(1:nrow(exposure_df), size = N, replace = TRUE, prob = exposure_df$prob)
      sev <- rlnorm(N, meanlog = exposure_df$meanlog[cells], sdlog = sdlog)
      sev <- sev * (1 + avg_inflation)^t
      disc <- 1 / (1 + spot_rates[min(t, max_year)])^t   # use spot rate for the year; beyond max_year use last rate
      total_pv <- total_pv + sum(sev * disc)
    }
  }
  if (i %% 100 == 0) setTxtProgressBar(pb, i)

close(pb)
  pv_loss_10y[i] <- total_pv
}

# Present value of premium stream for 10 years (annual premium paid at start of each year)
# For year 1 (t=0): discount factor = 1
# For year 2 (t=1): discount factor = 1/(1+spot_rates[1])
# For year 3 (t=2): discount factor = 1/(1+spot_rates[2])^2, etc.
disc_premium <- c(1, 1 / (1 + spot_rates[1:(T-1)])^(1:(T-1)))
pv_premium_10y <- sum(premium_annual * disc_premium)
net_revenue_10y <- pv_premium_10y - pv_loss_10y

#### SUMMARY STATISTICS
# --- Create loss and net revenue tables as data frames ---
loss_table <- data.frame(
  Horizon = c("1 year", "5 years", "10 years"),
  Mean = c(loss_1y["Mean"], loss_5y["Mean"], loss_10y["Mean"]),
  SD = c(loss_1y["SD"], loss_5y["SD"], loss_10y["SD"]),
  VaR_95 = c(loss_1y["VaR_95"], loss_5y["VaR_95"], loss_10y["VaR_95"]),
  VaR_99 = c(loss_1y["VaR_99"], loss_5y["VaR_99"], loss_10y["VaR_99"]),
  VaR_99.5 = c(loss_1y["VaR_99.5"], loss_5y["VaR_99.5"], loss_10y["VaR_99.5"]),
  VaR_99.9 = c(loss_1y["VaR_99.9"], loss_5y["VaR_99.9"], loss_10y["VaR_99.9"]),
  TVaR_99 = c(loss_1y["TVaR_99"], loss_5y["TVaR_99"], loss_10y["TVaR_99"]),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

nr_table <- data.frame(
  Horizon = c("1 year", "5 years", "10 years"),
  Mean = c(nr_1y["Mean"], nr_5y["Mean"], nr_10y["Mean"]),
  SD = c(nr_1y["SD"], nr_5y["SD"], nr_10y["SD"]),
  Prob_Negative = c(nr_1y["Prob_Negative"], nr_5y["Prob_Negative"], nr_10y["Prob_Negative"]),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# --- Print tables with formatting ---
cat("\n=== Present Value of Aggregate Losses (Đ million) ===\n")
print(round(loss_table[, c("Mean", "SD", "VaR_95", "VaR_99", "VaR_99.5", "VaR_99.9", "TVaR_99")], 2),
      row.names = FALSE)

cat("\n=== Net Revenue (Present Value, Đ million) ===\n")
print(round(nr_table[, c("Mean", "SD", "Prob_Negative")], 2),
      row.names = FALSE)

# Optionally, also display the full tables with horizon
cat("\nFull loss table:\n")
print(loss_table)

cat("\nFull net revenue table:\n")
print(nr_table)

###GIGGLYGOO
# ---- Loss summary table ----
loss_table <- data.frame(
  Horizon = c("1 year", "5 years", "10 years"),
  Mean = c(mean(pv_agg_loss_1y), mean(pv_loss_5y), mean(pv_loss_10y)),
  SD = c(sd(pv_agg_loss_1y), sd(pv_loss_5y), sd(pv_loss_10y)),
  VaR_95 = c(quantile(pv_agg_loss_1y, 0.95), quantile(pv_loss_5y, 0.95), quantile(pv_loss_10y, 0.95)),
  VaR_99 = c(quantile(pv_agg_loss_1y, 0.99), quantile(pv_loss_5y, 0.99), quantile(pv_loss_10y, 0.99)),
  VaR_99.5 = c(quantile(pv_agg_loss_1y, 0.995), quantile(pv_loss_5y, 0.995), quantile(pv_loss_10y, 0.995)),
  VaR_99.9 = c(quantile(pv_agg_loss_1y, 0.999), quantile(pv_loss_5y, 0.999), quantile(pv_loss_10y, 0.999)),
  TVaR_99 = c(
    mean(pv_agg_loss_1y[pv_agg_loss_1y > quantile(pv_agg_loss_1y, 0.99)]),
    mean(pv_loss_5y[pv_loss_5y > quantile(pv_loss_5y, 0.99)]),
    mean(pv_loss_10y[pv_loss_10y > quantile(pv_loss_10y, 0.99)])
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# ---- Net revenue summary table ----
nr_table <- data.frame(
  Horizon = c("1 year", "5 years", "10 years"),
  Mean = c(mean(net_revenue_1y), mean(net_revenue_5y), mean(net_revenue_10y)),
  SD = c(sd(net_revenue_1y), sd(net_revenue_5y), sd(net_revenue_10y)),
  Prob_Negative = c(mean(net_revenue_1y < 0), mean(net_revenue_5y < 0), mean(net_revenue_10y < 0)),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
show(loss_table)
show(nr_table)
```
```{r}
# ---- Stress testing function ----
# Parameters:
#   freq_mult: multiplier for total expected claims (default = 1)
#   sev_mult:  multiplier for severity (default = 1)
#   n_sim:     number of simulations (default = 100000)
# Returns a named vector with mean, sd, VaR_99, TVaR_99
stress_test <- function(freq_mult = 1, sev_mult = 1, n_sim = 100000) {
  # Adjust total expected claims and severity parameters
  stressed_lambda <- total_expected * freq_mult
  stressed_meanlog <- exposure_df$meanlog + log(sev_mult)   # because log(c*X) = log(c) + log(X)
  # sdlog remains unchanged
  
  set.seed(123)   # ensure reproducibility (optional: use same seed across scenarios)
  agg_loss <- numeric(n_sim)
  pb <- txtProgressBar(min = 0, max = n_sim, style = 3)
  for (i in 1:n_sim) {
    N <- rpois(1, stressed_lambda)
    if (N == 0) {
      agg_loss[i] <- 0
    } else {
      cells <- sample(1:nrow(exposure_df), size = N, replace = TRUE, prob = exposure_df$prob)
      sev <- rlnorm(N, meanlog = stressed_meanlog[cells], sdlog = sdlog)
      agg_loss[i] <- sum(sev)
    }
  }
  if (i %% 100 == 0) setTxtProgressBar(pb, i)

close(pb)
  # Apply inflation and discounting (same as 1‑year base)
  pv_loss <- agg_loss * (1 + avg_inflation) / (1 + spot_rates[1])
  
  # Compute statistics
  c(
    Mean = mean(pv_loss),
    SD = sd(pv_loss),
    VaR_99 = quantile(pv_loss, 0.99),
    TVaR_99 = mean(pv_loss[pv_loss > quantile(pv_loss, 0.99)])
  )
}

# ---- Run stress scenarios ----
scenarios <- list(
  Base = c(freq_mult = 1, sev_mult = 1),
  "+50% Frequency" = c(freq_mult = 1.5, sev_mult = 1),
  "+50% Severity" = c(freq_mult = 1, sev_mult = 1.5),
  "Combined (+50% each)" = c(freq_mult = 1.5, sev_mult = 1.5),
  "Catastrophic (2x each)" = c(freq_mult = 2, sev_mult = 2)
)

results <- data.frame(
  Scenario = names(scenarios),
  t(sapply(scenarios, function(params) {
    stress_test(freq_mult = params["freq_mult"], sev_mult = params["sev_mult"])
  }))
)

# ---- Print the table (rounded) ----
cat("\n=== Stress Testing Results (1‑year Present Value Losses, Đ million) ===\n")
print(round(results[, -1], 2), row.names = FALSE)








```
```{r}
# --- 1. Set annual premium to VaR_95 of the 1‑year undiscounted loss ---
premium_annual <- quantile(agg_loss, 0.95)   # from earlier simulation

# --- 2. Present value of premium for each horizon ---
# 1‑year: premium paid at inception → PV = premium_annual
premium_pv_1y <- premium_annual

# 5‑year: premium paid at start of each year
T5 <- 5
disc_premium_5y <- c(1, 1 / (1 + spot_rates[1:(T5-1)])^(1:(T5-1)))
premium_pv_5y <- sum(premium_annual * disc_premium_5y)

# 10‑year
T10 <- 10
disc_premium_10y <- c(1, 1 / (1 + spot_rates[1:(T10-1)])^(1:(T10-1)))
premium_pv_10y <- sum(premium_annual * disc_premium_10y)

# --- 3. Net revenue = premium PV – loss PV (already computed as pv_agg_loss_1y, pv_loss_5y, pv_loss_10y) ---
net_revenue_1y <- premium_pv_1y - pv_agg_loss_1y
net_revenue_5y <- premium_pv_5y - pv_loss_5y
net_revenue_10y <- premium_pv_10y - pv_loss_10y

# --- 4. Summary statistics ---
# Costs table (losses)
costs_table <- data.frame(
  Horizon = c("1 year", "5 years", "10 years"),
  Mean = c(mean(pv_agg_loss_1y), mean(pv_loss_5y), mean(pv_loss_10y)),
  SD = c(sd(pv_agg_loss_1y), sd(pv_loss_5y), sd(pv_loss_10y)),
  VaR_99 = c(quantile(pv_agg_loss_1y, 0.99), quantile(pv_loss_5y, 0.99), quantile(pv_loss_10y, 0.99)),
  TVaR_99 = c(
    mean(pv_agg_loss_1y[pv_agg_loss_1y > quantile(pv_agg_loss_1y, 0.99)]),
    mean(pv_loss_5y[pv_loss_5y > quantile(pv_loss_5y, 0.99)]),
    mean(pv_loss_10y[pv_loss_10y > quantile(pv_loss_10y, 0.99)])
  )
)

# Returns table (premium present value, deterministic)
returns_table <- data.frame(
  Horizon = c("1 year", "5 years", "10 years"),
  PV_Premium = c(premium_pv_1y, premium_pv_5y, premium_pv_10y)
)

# Net revenue table (including tail measures) – direct calculation
nr_table <- data.frame(
  Horizon = c("1 year", "5 years", "10 years"),
  Mean = c(mean(net_revenue_1y), mean(net_revenue_5y), mean(net_revenue_10y)),
  SD = c(sd(net_revenue_1y), sd(net_revenue_5y), sd(net_revenue_10y)),
  Prob_Negative = c(mean(net_revenue_1y < 0), mean(net_revenue_5y < 0), mean(net_revenue_10y < 0)),
  VaR_99_Left = c(
    quantile(net_revenue_1y, 0.01),
    quantile(net_revenue_5y, 0.01),
    quantile(net_revenue_10y, 0.01)
  ),
  TVaR_99_Left = c(
    mean(net_revenue_1y[net_revenue_1y < quantile(net_revenue_1y, 0.01)]),
    mean(net_revenue_5y[net_revenue_5y < quantile(net_revenue_5y, 0.01)]),
    mean(net_revenue_10y[net_revenue_10y < quantile(net_revenue_10y, 0.01)])
  )
)

# --- 5. Print tables (rounded, safely handling non‑numeric columns) ---
print_rounded <- function(df, digits = 2) {
  df_rounded <- df
  num_cols <- sapply(df_rounded, is.numeric)
  if (any(num_cols)) {
    df_rounded[, num_cols] <- round(df_rounded[, num_cols], digits)
  }
  print(df_rounded, row.names = FALSE)
}

cat("\n=== Costs (Present Value of Aggregate Losses, Đ million) ===\n")
print_rounded(costs_table)

cat("\n=== Returns (Present Value of Premium, Đ million) ===\n")
print_rounded(returns_table)

cat("\n=== Net Revenue (Present Value, Đ million) ===\n")
print_rounded(nr_table)

```











```{r}
## Risk Identification
# ---- 1. Compute expected loss per cell ----
# Expected severity = exp(meanlog + 0.5 * sdlog^2)
exposure_df$expected_severity <- exp(exposure_df$meanlog + 0.5 * exposure_df$sdlog^2)

# Expected total loss = exposure * lambda * expected_severity
exposure_df$expected_loss <- exposure_df$exposure * exposure_df$lambda * exposure_df$expected_severity

# ---- 2. Create a threat table sorted by expected loss ----
# Use dplyr::select to avoid conflicts
threat_table <- exposure_df %>%
  dplyr::select(solar_system2, equipment_type2, age_band, exposure, lambda, expected_severity, expected_loss) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(expected_loss))

# ---- 3. Display top threats overall ----
cat("\n=== Top 10 Threats (Overall) ===\n")
print(head(threat_table, 10))

# ---- 4. Top threats by solar system ----
cat("\n=== Top 5 Threats per Solar System ===\n")
threat_by_system <- threat_table %>%
  group_by(solar_system2) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = expected_loss, n = 5) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(solar_system2, desc(expected_loss))

print(threat_by_system)

## Grouped
# ---- Summary by solar system ----
summary_by_system <- exposure_df %>%
  group_by(solar_system2) %>%
  summarise(
    Total_Expected_Loss = sum(expected_loss),
    Total_Exposure = sum(exposure),
    # Top equipment type (by total expected loss)
    Top_Equipment = equipment_type2[which.max(expected_loss)],
    # Dominant age band (by total expected loss)
    Dominant_Age = age_band[which.max(expected_loss)]
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Print summary
print(summary_by_system)

```

```{r}

##Product Design- Determining a deductible
  
## Product Design - Determining a deductible

# ---- 1. Load historical severity data ----
# Assume Severity data frame exists with columns: claim_amount, solar_system2 (cleaned)
sev_pos <- Severity[Severity$claim_amount > 0, ]

# ---- Remove rows with NA in solar_system2 ----
sev_pos <- sev_pos[!is.na(sev_pos$solar_system2), ]

# ---- 2. Define systems and percentiles ----
systems <- c("Helionis Cluster", "Epsilon", "Zeta")
pct <- c(0.10, 0.20, 0.25, 0.30, 0.50, 0.75, 0.90, 0.95, 0.99)

# ---- 3. Compute percentiles per system ----
quantiles_by_system <- lapply(systems, function(sys) {
  claims <- sev_pos$claim_amount[sev_pos$solar_system2 == sys]
  if(length(claims) == 0) return(rep(NA, length(pct)))
  quantile(claims, probs = pct, na.rm = TRUE)
})
names(quantiles_by_system) <- systems

# ---- 4. Display percentiles table ----
quantiles_df <- data.frame(
  Percentile = pct,
  Helionis_Cluster = quantiles_by_system[["Helionis Cluster"]],
  Epsilon = quantiles_by_system[["Epsilon"]],
  Zeta = quantiles_by_system[["Zeta"]]
)

cat("\n=== Claim Amount Percentiles by Solar System (Đ) ===\n")
print(round(quantiles_df, 0))

# ---- 5. Propose deductibles as the 20th percentile ----
deductibles <- data.frame(
  solar_system2 = systems,
  deductible = c(quantiles_by_system[["Helionis Cluster"]]["20%"],
                 quantiles_by_system[["Epsilon"]]["20%"],
                 quantiles_by_system[["Zeta"]]["20%"])
)

cat("\n=== Proposed Per‑Claim Deductibles by Solar System (20th percentile) ===\n")
print(deductibles)

# ---- 6. Visualise the three distributions ----
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(sev_pos, aes(x = claim_amount, fill = solar_system2)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = 0.6, position = "identity") +
  facet_wrap(~ solar_system2, scales = "free") +   # <-- corrected facet variable
  geom_vline(data = deductibles, aes(xintercept = deductible), color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Historical Claim Severity Distributions by Solar System",
       x = "Claim Amount (Đ)", y = "Frequency") +
  theme_minimal()

ggplot(sev_pos, aes(x = claim_amount, color = solar_system2, fill = solar_system2)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.3, adjust = 1.5) +
  geom_vline(data = deductibles, aes(xintercept = deductible, color = solar_system2),
             linetype = "dashed", size = 0.8) +
  labs(title = "Claim Severity Distributions by Solar System",
       x = "Claim Amount (Đ)", y = "Density") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Helionis Cluster" = "blue", "Epsilon" = "red", "Zeta" = "green")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Helionis Cluster" = "blue", "Epsilon" = "red", "Zeta" = "green")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  xlim(0, quantile(sev_pos$claim_amount, 0.99))   # zoom to 99th percentile for readability
```


```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# ---- 1. Prepare historical data per system ----
sev_pos <- Severity[Severity$claim_amount > 0 & !is.na(Severity$solar_system2), ]
systems <- c("Helionis Cluster", "Epsilon", "Zeta")

# ---- 2. Empirical LER function ----
empirical_LER <- function(claims, deductible) {
  if (deductible <= 0) return(0)
  mean_claims <- mean(claims)
  limited_mean <- mean(pmin(claims, deductible))
  limited_mean / mean_claims
}

# ---- 3. Compute LER curves up to 2 million ----
d_range <- seq(0, 2e6, by = 50000)
ler_data <- data.frame()

for (sys in systems) {
  claims <- sev_pos$claim_amount[sev_pos$solar_system2 == sys]
  if (length(claims) > 0) {
    for (d in d_range) {
      LER <- empirical_LER(claims, d)
      ler_data <- rbind(ler_data, data.frame(system = sys, deductible = d, LER = LER))
    }
  }
}

# ---- 4. Compute marginal reduction (ΔLER per million Đ) ----
marginal_data <- ler_data %>%
  group_by(system) %>%
  arrange(deductible) %>%
  mutate(
    delta_LER = LER - lag(LER),
    delta_d = deductible - lag(deductible),
    marginal_per_million = delta_LER / (delta_d / 1e6)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# ---- 5. Proposed deductibles ----
proposed <- data.frame(
  system = systems,
  deductible = c(1e6, 1.5e6, 2e6)
)

proposed$LER <- NA
for (i in 1:nrow(proposed)) {
  sys <- proposed$system[i]
  d <- proposed$deductible[i]
  claims <- sev_pos$claim_amount[sev_pos$solar_system2 == sys]
  if (length(claims) > 0) {
    proposed$LER[i] <- empirical_LER(claims, d)
  }
}

# Get marginal values at proposed deductibles
marginal_proposed <- marginal_data %>%
  semi_join(proposed, by = c("system", "deductible")) %>%
  select(system, deductible, marginal_per_million)

# ---- 6. Plots ----
# LER plot
p1 <- ggplot(ler_data, aes(x = deductible / 1e6, y = LER, color = system, group = system)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2) +
  geom_point(data = proposed, aes(x = deductible / 1e6, y = LER), size = 3, shape = 18) +
  geom_text(data = proposed, aes(label = paste0(round(LER*100,1), "%")),
            vjust = -1, hjust = -0.2, size = 4) +
  labs(title = "Loss Elimination Ratio",
       x = "Deductible (Đ million)", y = "Proportion of Expected Loss Eliminated") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Helionis Cluster" = "blue", "Epsilon" = "red", "Zeta" = "green")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 2)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent)

# Marginal reduction plot
p2 <- ggplot(marginal_data, aes(x = deductible / 1e6, y = marginal_per_million, color = system, group = system)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2) +
  geom_point(data = marginal_proposed, aes(x = deductible / 1e6, y = marginal_per_million), size = 3, shape = 18) +
  geom_text(data = marginal_proposed, aes(label = paste0(round(marginal_per_million, 3), " %/MĐ")),
            vjust = -1, hjust = -0.2, size = 3) +
  labs(title = "Marginal Reduction in Expected Loss",
       x = "Deductible (Đ million)", y = "Percentage point reduction per Đ1M increase") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Helionis Cluster" = "blue", "Epsilon" = "red", "Zeta" = "green")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 2)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.5) +
  annotate("text", x = 1.8, y = 1.1, label = "Flattening region", size = 3)

# Display plots (you can run them one after the other, or use gridExtra if you want them side by side)
print(p1)
print(p2)

# ---- Choose your step size (in Đ) ----
step <- 100000   # 100,000 Đ
threshold <- 0.1  # percent per 100,000 Đ

# ---- Recompute d_range with the chosen step ----
d_range <- seq(0, 2e6, by = step)

# (Re‑run the LER calculation with the new d_range, then compute marginal per step)
marginal_data <- ler_data %>%
  group_by(system) %>%
  arrange(deductible) %>%
  mutate(
    delta_LER = LER - lag(LER),
    delta_d = deductible - lag(deductible),
    marginal_per_step = delta_LER / (delta_d / step)   # percentage point per step unit
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Find where marginal drops below threshold
flattening_points <- marginal_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(marginal_per_step)) %>%
  group_by(system) %>%
  filter(marginal_per_step <= threshold) %>%
  slice(1) %>%
  select(system, deductible, marginal_per_step)

print(flattening_points)

```

```{r}

# Corrected LER function: proportion of expected loss eliminated by deductible
eliminated_proportion <- function(claims, deductible) {
  if (deductible <= 0) return(0)
  mean_claims <- mean(claims)
  limited_mean <- mean(pmin(claims, deductible))
  1 - limited_mean / mean_claims
}

# Compute at flattening points
flattening <- data.frame(
  system = c("Helionis Cluster", "Epsilon", "Zeta"),
  deductible = c(200000, 200000, 250000)
)
flattening$LER <- mapply(function(s, d) {
  claims <- sev_pos$claim_amount[sev_pos$solar_system2 == s]
  eliminated_proportion(claims, d)
}, flattening$system, flattening$deductible)

# Show results
print(flattening)

```




```{r}
# ---- 1. Per‑occurrence limit (based on historical severity) ----
# Use the 99th percentile of all positive claim amounts
sev_all <- Severity$claim_amount[Severity$claim_amount > 0]
per_occ_limit <- quantile(sev_all, 0.99, na.rm = TRUE)
cat("Recommended per‑occurrence limit (99th percentile): Đ", round(per_occ_limit, 0), "\n")

# ---- 2. Aggregate annual limit (based on simulation with deductibles) ----
# Re‑run the simulation with the chosen deductibles (system‑specific)
# We already have exposure_df with lambda, meanlog, sdlog, and deductibles per system
# We need to simulate paid losses: for each claim, paid = max(0, severity - deductible)

# Set deductibles per system (from earlier analysis)
deductible_df <- data.frame(
  solar_system2 = c("Helionis Cluster", "Epsilon", "Zeta"),
  deductible = c(200000, 200000, 250000)
)
exposure_df <- exposure_df %>% left_join(deductible_df, by = "solar_system2")

# Simulation parameters
n_sim <- 10000
total_expected <- sum(exposure_df$exposure * exposure_df$lambda)
cell_probs <- (exposure_df$exposure * exposure_df$lambda) / total_expected

set.seed(123)
paid_loss <- numeric(n_sim)

for (i in 1:n_sim) {
  N <- rpois(1, total_expected)
  if (N == 0) {
    paid_loss[i] <- 0
  } else {
    cells <- sample(1:nrow(exposure_df), size = N, replace = TRUE, prob = cell_probs)
    sev <- rlnorm(N, meanlog = exposure_df$meanlog[cells], sdlog = exposure_df$sdlog[cells])
    deduct <- exposure_df$deductible[cells]
    paid <- pmax(sev - deduct, 0)
    # Apply per‑occurrence limit (if any claim exceeds the limit, cap it)
    # For now, we don't apply a per‑occurrence limit in this simulation;
    # we will later cap at the chosen limit.
    paid_loss[i] <- sum(paid)
  }
}

# Now choose aggregate limit as a high percentile of the paid loss distribution
agg_limit <- quantile(paid_loss, 0.995)   # 99.5th percentile
cat("Recommended aggregate annual limit (99.5% VaR of paid losses): Đ", round(agg_limit, 0), "\n")

# Optionally, you can also compute the 99.9th percentile for a more conservative limit
agg_limit_extreme <- quantile(paid_loss, 0.999)
cat("Extreme aggregate limit (99.9% VaR): Đ", round(agg_limit_extreme, 0), "\n")

```
